summary: Jeff Atwood announces his decision to add advertising to Coding Horror after years of resistance, but frames it as an ethical compromise rather than a sellout. He acknowledges the legitimate criticisms of blog advertising—loss of credibility, design degradation, and professionalism concerns—but accepts that advertising has become the inevitable online revenue model. The post outlines strategies for responsible advertising: being selective with ad placement, respecting readers through time-delayed ads and full RSS feeds, and preferring direct sponsor relationships over generic ad networks. His key justification is committing to donate $5,000 of ad revenue to .NET open source projects, with Microsoft matching the contribution.
key_points: Advertising has won so completely online that alternative revenue models are essentially non-viable for most websites
Direct sponsor relationships give bloggers maximum control over what appears on their site, unlike automated ad networks
Respecting readers means being selective: use heat maps for placement, delay ads on new content, keep RSS feeds ad-free
The hierarchy of blog monetization places Google AdSense at the bottom and direct fixed sponsors at the top
Giving back to the community through ad revenue donations transforms advertising from a necessary evil into a positive contribution
money_quotes: Kids are so used to being blasted with ads that they don't notice them.
Advertising sucks. But you know what else sucks? When people point out how stupid you are to throw away five figures worth of potential income.
Is it possible to advertise responsibly, with respect for your audience – and yourself?
Don't use the Ronco spray-on advertising approach – e.g., indiscriminately placing low-value Google AdSense units in every nook and cranny of your page.
It's an open secret amongst bloggers that the blog comments are often better than the original blog post, and it's because the community collectively knows far more than you or I will ever know.
Giving back to the community is what makes the pain of advertising worthwhile.
key_insight: The ethical path to blog monetization isn't avoiding advertising entirely, but rather taking maximum control over ad placement, respecting your readers' experience, and channeling the revenue back into the community that made your platform possible.
